background: We propose to draw boxplots for the air sample data presented in prior illustrations (see Table 4.2).

task.query: Data for site 1 is: $\begin{array}{llllllll}22 & 24 & 28 & 32 & 36 & 38 & 42 & 68\end{array}$. What is the five-point summary, and how is the boxplot constructed?
task.answer: The five-point summary (determined in a prior illustration) is: 22, 26, 34, 40, 68. Therefore, the box extends from 26 to 40. A line for the median is drawn in the box at 34.

task.query: For site 1, calculate the interquartile range (IQR) and determine the fences for outliers. Are there any outliers?
task.answer: $\mathrm{IQR}=40-26=14$. Therefore, Fence $_{\text {Lower }}=26-(1.5)(14)=5$ and Fence $_{\text {Upper }}=40+(1.5)(14)=61$. There are no values below the lower fence. There is one value (68) above the upper fence. The upper outside value of 68 is plotted as a separate point above the box.

task.query: For site 1, identify the inside values and describe how the whiskers are drawn.
task.answer: The smallest value still inside the fence (lower inside value) is 22. A whisker is drawn from Q1 to this inside value. The upper inside value is 42. A whisker is drawn from Q3 to this inside value.

task.query: Data for site 2 is: $\begin{array}{llllllll}32 & 33 & 34 & 36 & 36 & 38 & 39 & 40\end{array}$. What is the five-point summary, and how is the boxplot constructed?
task.answer: The five-point summary is $32,33.5,36,38.5,40$. The box extends from 33.5 to 38.5. A line for the median is drawn inside the box at 36.

task.query: For site 2, calculate the interquartile range (IQR) and determine the fences for outliers. Are there any outliers?
task.answer: $\mathrm{IQR}=38.5-33.5=5$. Fence $_{\text {Lower }}=33.5-(1.5)(5)=26$. Fence $_{\text {Upper }}=38.5+(1.5)(5)=46$. There are no outside values in this group.

task.query: For site 2, identify the inside values and describe how the whiskers are drawn.
task.answer: The lower inside value is 32. A whisker is drawn from Q1 to this lower inside value. The upper inside value is 42. A whisker is drawn from Q3 to this upper inside value.

task.query: Compare the side-by-side boxplots for site 1 and site 2. What do they reveal about the central locations and spreads?
task.answer: Figure 4.5 shows the boxplot for site 1 on the left and site 2 on the right. The side-by-side boxplots in this figure show site 1 and site 2 have similar central locations but different spreads.
